ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for seasoned ground chicken patty; A0CKP (chicken meat products) used as closest defensible parent anchor for processed formed poultry product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken patty as a food item; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chicken patty weighs approximately 85 g and is typically served as a burger patty or as a standalone protein component in a meal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken patty — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for chicken patty (F11070004). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken patty (NDB 05298) as the closest approved-source analogue, as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — analogous estimate based on mixed meat patty with breadcrumb binder
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a seasoned chicken patty. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on analogous mixed meat patty with breadcrumb binder from the University of Sydney GI Database. The breadcrumb component (moderate GI ~65) is diluted by the dominant protein and fat matrix, yielding a blended low GI. Glycemic load per 85 g serving = (48 × 7.6 × 0.85) / 100 ≈ 3.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 243 mg/100 g with a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio (potassium 260 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one patty (85 g, ~207 mg sodium) and avoid adding high-sodium condiments such as ketchup or BBQ sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake; grilling rather than pan-frying is preferred to avoid additional fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High protein (18 g/100 g) and low sugar (0.3 g/100 g) with moderate low-GI carbohydrate (7.6 g/100 g, estimated GI ~48)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high protein and fat content of the chicken patty slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose rise; individuals with type 2 diabetes should pair it with non-starchy vegetables rather than refined-carbohydrate buns, and avoid high-sugar condiments to maintain glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 2.8 g/100 g and sodium 243 mg/100 g; cholesterol 35 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease can include a grilled chicken patty in moderation as saturated fat (≈2.8 g/100 g) is below the 5 g threshold and cholesterol is modest at 35 mg/100 g; however, sodium should be managed by avoiding added sauces, and preparation method should favour grilling or baking over deep-frying to prevent additional saturated and trans fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A chicken patty is a processed poultry product made from seasoned ground (minced) chicken meat that is formed into a flat, round disc shape and cooked by grilling, pan-frying, or baking. The patty typically contains breadcrumbs as a binder, along with salt, pepper, garlic, and herbs such as parsley or thyme, giving it a savoury, mildly herbed flavour with a firm yet moist texture. Per 100 g, it provides 206 kcal, 18 g protein, 11.5 g fat (approximately 2.8 g saturated), 7.6 g carbohydrate, 0.3 g sugar, 1.3 g dietary fibre, and 243 mg sodium. It is a high-protein, moderate-fat food with a low estimated glycaemic index (~48) due to the dominant protein-fat matrix diluting the breadcrumb carbohydrate. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes when served without refined-carbohydrate accompaniments, and is acceptable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease in moderate portions without high-sodium condiments. It is commonly served as a burger patty, in a sandwich, or alongside salad or vegetables as a standalone protein. The product is typically halal-certified when prepared with halal-certified chicken and is widely available in Singapore hawker centres, fast-food outlets, and home kitchens.
common_user_queries: how many calories in a chicken patty
is chicken patty good for diabetes
chicken patty vs beef patty nutrition
can I eat chicken patty with hypertension

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11070004) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 2.8 g/100 g based on typical chicken patty composition consistent with USDA FoodData Central reference data for similar products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat), full micronutrient panel (vitamins C, A, B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, D, E), and mineral/moisture data (phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) for this processed chicken patty are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11070004 as of 2026, and no equivalent complete panel was found in USDA FoodData Central for an exact match. Saturated fat value of 2.8 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken patty (NDB 05298) as the closest approved-source analogue. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for seasoned chicken patty was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. This food is not a Singaporean dish and therefore multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply; it is classified as an international processed food product.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Chicken Patty', a precise canonical English name. This is an international processed food product; no mandatory local-language names apply under G6 multilingual rules.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 206.0, protein_g 18.0, fat_g 11.5, carbohydrate_g 7.6 — all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo F11070004 with confidence 0.95.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 1.3, sodium_mg 243.0, saturated_fat_g 2.8 (estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogue NDB 05298), sugar_g 0.3 — all present and numeric.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 48 (analogous estimate, low category), gi_category 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving 3.1, gi_source documented with University of Sydney GI Database framework and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed reference. Estimation basis clearly documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for chicken patty (FOODON_03302757). FoodEx2 code A0CKP used with match_type 'closest_parent' and gap note explaining the choice. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented. foodex2_match_type corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' per valid enumeration.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance, and serving context.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (low), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 7 English-language variants. All strings in aliases_by_language.english verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. No mandatory multilingual aliases required for this non-Singaporean international food.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Documented all -1 sentinel fields in the 'notes' section with explicit explanation of unavailability from HPB Singapore Foodo F11070004 and USDA FoodData Central as of 2026.
Iteration 2: Corrected foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' (invalid value) to 'closest_parent' (valid enumeration per template).

Iteration 3: Expanded -1 documentation in 'notes' to individually name every field set to -1 and state the reason data is unavailable, satisfying g3_numeric_hygiene sentinel documentation requirement explicitly.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Chicken Patty (F11070004) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo with high confidence. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogue (NDB 05298). GI replaced from invalid input value of 0 to an analogous estimate of 48 (low) with full documentation. All three required health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance including Type 2 Diabetes. Ontology anchors are defensible and honest with foodex2_match_type corrected to 'closest_parent'. All 18 fields set to -1 are individually named in notes with reason for unavailability and source consulted. No multilingual naming requirement applies as this is an international processed food product.
